concept: Quiet / Making Little Sound / Peaceful Stillness
description: Making little or no noise; characterized by calm and silence. 'Maeli' names the quality of soft presence—the stillness that allows listening, the calm that enables peace.
sound_symbolism: The word settles into stillness. 'Ma' grounds with gentle presence, noise fading. The vowel 'e' opens to silence, space clearing. Then 'li' rests lightly, quiet complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'maeli lokue' (quiet place). Related to 'thiro' (serene) but emphasizes absence of sound rather than inner peace.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Quiet enables listening—to others, to self, to what arises in stillness.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Quiet presence supports others' expression—not filling space allows others to speak.
